articles.title: Android 17 Blocks Non-Accessibility Apps from Accessibility API to Prevent Malware Abuse
articles.summary: Google is testing a new security feature as part of Android Advanced Protection Mode (AAPM) that prevents certain kinds of apps from using the accessibility services API. The change, incorporated in Android 17 Beta 2, was first reported by Android Authority last week. AAPM was in

articles.title: These 15 Amazon Spring Sale Tech Deals Are Actually Good. WWe Checked the Price History (2026)
articles.summary: This sale spans almost an entire week, and it’s full of fake “deals.” We hunted to find truly good prices on gear we’ve tested.

articles.title: The billionaires made a promise — now some want out
articles.summary: In 2010, Warren Buffett and Bill Gates launched a disarmingly simple campaign they called the Giving Pledge: a public commitment, open to the world s wealthiest people, to give away more than half their fortune during their lifetime or upon their death. The moment seemed to call

articles.title: Netflix’s ‘Frankenstein’ wins three Oscars, ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ wins two
articles.summary: “Frankenstein” won for Best Production Design, Best Costume Design, and Best Makeup and Hairstyling, while “KPop Demon Hunters” won for Best Animated Feature and Best Original Song.

articles.title: Google, Accel India accelerator chooses 5 startups and none are ‘AI wrappers’
articles.summary: Google and Accel say about 70% of AI startup pitches tied to India were "wrappers" as they reviewed more than 4,000 applications for their Atoms cohort.

articles.title: OpenAI says ChatGPT ads are not rolling out globally for now
articles.summary: OpenAI told BleepingComputer that ChatGPT ads on Free and Go plans are not yet rolling out outside the United States, even though some users noticed references to ads in the updated privacy policy. [...]

articles.title: ByteDance reportedly pauses global launch of its Seedance 2.0 video generator
articles.summary: The company is reportedly delaying the launch as its engineers and lawyers work to avert further legal issues.

articles.title: Aether OS is a computer in a browser built for the AT Protocol
articles.summary: Aether OS puts a full-fledged desktop in your browser that ties directly into the AT Protocol . That means it connects to your Bluesky account and other public records. It offers a pretty full suite of apps , 42 in total, covering text editing, task management, and social media.

articles.title: Lawyer behind AI psychosis cases warns of mass casualty risks
articles.summary: AI chatbots have been linked to suicides for years. Now one lawyer says they are showing up in mass casualty cases too, and the technology is moving faster than the safeguards.

articles.title: Unacademy to be acquired by upGrad in share-swap deal as India’s edtech sector consolidates
articles.summary: The deal comes after Unacademy’s valuation plunged from $3.5B to under $500M, as India’s once-booming edtech sector struggles.

articles.title: An engineering thesis disguised as a coupe: A history of the Honda Prelude
articles.summary: The Honda Prelude was never simply a car. It was an engineering thesis disguised as a coupe: compact, disciplined, and unapologetically technical. At its best, it distilled Honda’s faith in precision manufacturing and clever packaging into something accessible and aspirational. I

articles.title: Microsoft releases Windows 11 OOB hotpatch to fix RRAS RCE flaw
articles.summary: Microsoft has released an out-of-band (OOB) update to fix a security vulnerabilities affecting Windows 11 Enterprise devices that receive hotpatch updates instead of the regular Patch Tuesday cumulative updates. [...]
